programs.name: Medi-Cal Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: These programs help pay Medicare Part A/B premiums, deductibles, coinsurance, and copays for low-income beneficiaries. QMB covers full costs, SLMB/QI cover premiums only.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Medicare enrollees; income limits vary (e.g., tied to Medi-Cal thresholds); serves 1.7M in CA.[3][6]
programs.benefit_value: Covers premiums/out-of-pocket up to thousands annually; essential for affordability.[3]

programs.name: Multipurpose Senior Services Program (MSSP) Waiver
programs.what_it_does: The MSSP Waiver provides home and community-based services (HCBS) like personal care, homemaker services, home modifications, and emergency response to prevent nursing home placement. It supports safe aging in place and includes telehealth options.[1][4]
programs.who_its_for: Medi-Cal eligible individuals aged 60+ at risk of nursing facility level of care, residing in counties with MSSP sites.[4]
programs.benefit_value: HCBS as alternative to institutional care; funded by state/federal Medicaid; enrollment limited to one waiver.[4]

programs.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)
programs.what_it_does: PACE provides comprehensive medical and social services to frail seniors in community settings, including adult day health care, transportation, and home care to avoid nursing homes. It coordinates all Medicare and Medicaid-covered services plus extras.
programs.who_its_for: Medi-Cal/Medicare eligible nursing home-certifiable seniors aged 55+ able to live safely in community.
programs.benefit_value: Full capitation coverage of health/LTC needs; available in select CA counties.

programs.name: CalFresh
programs.what_it_does: CalFresh (California's SNAP) provides monthly EBT card benefits for groceries to low-income households. It helps seniors afford nutritious food.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ with income/resources below limits; automatic eligibility for SSI recipients in some cases.
programs.benefit_value: Average $200-300/month per person; higher for households.

programs.name: LIHEAP
programs.what_it_does: The Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program provides grants for heating/cooling bills and crisis assistance. It prevents utility shutoffs for vulnerable residents.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households including seniors 60+ meeting income guidelines (e.g., <150% FPL).

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
programs.what_it_does: WAP funds energy efficiency upgrades like insulation, sealing, and efficient appliances to reduce utility costs. It improves home safety and comfort for low-income residents.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households, prioritizing seniors, disabled, and families with children.
programs.benefit_value: Free home improvements worth $5,000-$10,000+ per household.

programs.name: Health Insurance Counseling & Advocacy Program (HICAP)
programs.what_it_does: HICAP offers free, unbiased counseling on Medicare, Medi-Cal, and supplemental insurance options. It assists with appeals, enrollment, and cost-saving strategies.[7]
programs.who_its_for: Medicare beneficiaries, families, caregivers; all ages 65+ statewide via AAAs.
programs.benefit_value: Free one-on-one counseling; saves thousands via proper enrollment.

programs.name: Meals on Wheels (via AAAs)
programs.what_it_does: Provides home-delivered nutritious meals to homebound seniors to promote health and reduce isolation. Often includes safety check-ins.
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors 60+ unable to prepare meals, low-income prioritized; via local AAAs.[7]

programs.name: Family Caregiver Support Program
programs.what_it_does: Offers respite care, counseling, training, and supplies to family caregivers of seniors. Helps prevent caregiver burnout.
programs.who_its_for: Unpaid family caregivers of adults 60+ or younger with Alzheimer's; via AAAs.[7]

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides part-time paid community service jobs with training for employability. Helps low-income seniors transition to unsubsidized work.
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage jobs (20 hrs/wk) + support services.

programs.name: Legal Services for Seniors (via AAAs)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal advice, representation for issues like housing, benefits, abuse, and conservatorships. Operated through Area Agencies on Aging.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ facing legal barriers; priority for most vulnerable.[7]
programs.benefit_value: Free legal aid worth thousands; statewide access.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in long-term care facilities, investigating complaints and ensuring quality care. Provides education and support to resolve issues.
programs.who_its_for: Residents/families in nursing homes, RCFEs, and board & care; statewide via AAAs.[7]
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy; protects rights of 100,000+ residents.

programs.name: State Supplementary Payment (SSP)
programs.what_it_does: SSP provides additional monthly cash payments to eligible Supplemental Security Income (SSI) recipients to help cover essential living expenses like food and housing. It supplements the federal SSI benefit, making California's total payment higher than the federal amount alone.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors aged 65 or older, blind, or disabled individuals receiving SSI.
programs.whats_unique: California adds its own state-funded supplement to federal SSI, which is higher than in most states and automatically provides Medi-Cal eligibility.
programs.benefit_value: Up to approximately $943 per month combined with federal SSI (as of 2020 data)

programs.name: Property Tax Postponement Program
programs.what_it_does: This program allows eligible homeowners to defer property tax payments, with the state covering the taxes as an interest-free loan. The deferred amount plus interest is repaid when the home is sold or transferred.
programs.who_its_for: Homeowners aged 62 or older with low income and limited assets.
programs.whats_unique: California's unique deferral option postpones taxes until sale or transfer, unlike standard exemptions or reductions in other states.
programs.benefit_value: Defers 100% of property taxes owed, saving immediate cash flow

programs.name: In-Home Supportive Services (IHSS)
programs.what_it_does: IHSS provides personal care aides to assist with daily tasks like bathing, dressing, housekeeping, and meal preparation. It enables seniors to remain safely in their homes rather than entering nursing facilities.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors and disabled adults eligible for Medi-Cal who need assistance to stay independent at home.
programs.whats_unique: California's Medi-Cal-funded program is one of the largest state home care systems, covering over 1.1 million in long-term services and emphasizing community-based care over institutionalization.
programs.benefit_value: Up to 283 hours of paid care per month, valued at average $137,000/year equivalent for long-term needs

programs.name: Caregiver Resource Centers (CRC)
programs.what_it_does: CRCs offer support to family caregivers including respite care, counseling, support groups, training, and care planning. Services are provided at low or no cost to help caregivers manage conditions like Alzheimer's and Parkinson's.
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of older adults with Alzheimer's, stroke, Parkinson's, or other disorders.
programs.whats_unique: California is the first state with a comprehensive, statewide network of CRCs accessible to every resident.
programs.benefit_value: Free or low-cost services including respite care and counseling
